summary: Do not ship as-is: the fixer can inject and execute arbitrary engine step types with only minimal shape validation, which turns a model patch into command/connector/API execution authority.

findings.title: Fixer patches can introduce arbitrary executable step types without schema or allowlist validation
findings.recommendation: Restrict fixer-generated `newStep` to a tightly validated browser-only schema and reject all non-browser step types. Enforce that in `validatePatch` and again before execution in the engine, so fixer patches cannot create `local_command`, `connector`, `api_call`, or `sub_automation` steps.

findings.title: Engine executes fixer-inserted steps through the normal dispatcher, enabling shell, connector, and network actions from a model patch
findings.recommendation: Before applying or executing a patch, verify the patched step at `i` is an allowed remediation step type and schema. If the fixer is intended only for page recovery, constrain execution to `browser`/`verify`/`navigate` repair actions and fail closed on anything else.
